title: 10 Herbal Teas to Calm and Smooth Frizzy Hair
intro_desc: Herbal teas can be beneficial for frizzy hair due to their natural properties that help to control moisture and reduce frizz. Two herbs commonly used in hair care are Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, and Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemon grass. Rosemary is rich in antioxidants that help to protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizz and dryness. It also helps to improve blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. Lemon grass, on the other hand, has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to control scalp infections and irritation, which can lead to frizz and unmanageable hair.  Frizzy hair can be frustrating and affect a person's self-confidence, making everyday tasks like getting dressed and going out in public more difficult. It can also lead to hair breakage and split ends, which can be costly and time-consuming to repair. By controlling frizz, you can improve your overall appearance and feel more comfortable in your own skin. Additionally, healthy hair can reduce stress and anxiety caused by hair-related problems, leading to a better quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These help to protect your hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can make your hair look frizzy and unmanageable. Rosemary tea also helps to improve blood circulation on your scalp, which brings more oxygen and nutrients to your hair follicles. This helps to strengthen your hair and make it less prone to breakage, which can contribute to frizz. The tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce dandruff and itchiness on the scalp, which can make your hair look frizzy. Additionally, rosemary tea has natural moisturizing properties that help to lock in moisture and keep your hair hydrated, which is especially important for people with dry or damaged hair that tends to frizz.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for frizzy hair because it helps to calm and smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine, thanks to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that protect the hair from damage and keep it healthy and strong.

Carnosic acid is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help to protect the hair cuticles and reduce damage caused by free radicals, resulting in smoother and more manageable locks.

Oleanolic acid is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help to protect the hair from damage, reduce inflammation, and balance the scalp's natural oil production, resulting in smoother and less frizzy hair.
Caffeic acid is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help protect hair from damage, smoothing its cuticle and reducing frizz.

Quercetin is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation in the scalp, leading to smoother and more manageable hair.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for frizzy hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal teas that can help with frizzy hair, start by gathering fresh or dried Thymus serpyllum leaves. You'll need about one teaspoon of the leaves for every cup of boiling water. If using fresh leaves, simply chop them finely and add them to a saucepan. If using dried leaves, you can directly add them to the saucepan. Pour in boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to infuse their properties into the water. Strain the tea into a cup, and discard the leaves. Thymus serpyllum has natural astringent properties that can help balance the scalp's pH and reduce frizz. To use the tea for hair, pour the cooled tea over your hair after shampooing, or use it as a final rinse before styling. Leave the tea in for 5-10 minutes, then rinse thoroughly with warm water. This can help smooth the hair cuticles, reduce frizz, and add shine to your hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, is a natural remedy for frizzy hair. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals. This damage can lead to frizz, dryness, and breakage. Lemongrass tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce irritation and soothe the scalp. When you drink lemongrass tea, the antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds are absorbed into the body, where they can help to strengthen hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth. Additionally, lemongrass tea has a natural cooling effect, which can help to reduce dandruff and itchiness on the scalp, further reducing frizz. Some people also use lemongrass tea as a final rinse after shampooing, as it can help to seal the cuticle and lock in moisture, leaving hair smoother and less prone to frizz.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Limonene is good for frizzy hair because it helps to protect the hair from damage by its antioxidant properties, which also help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz caused by dryness and breakage.
Linalool helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and sealing the hair cuticles, resulting in a more manageable and silky appearance.
Geraniol is good for frizzy hair because it helps to lock in moisture and reduce frizz by forming a protective barrier on the hair shaft.

Polyphenols, also known as wild thyme, are good for frizzy hair because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help lock in moisture, reduce damage, and smooth the hair cuticle, leaving it looking healthier and more manageable.
Tannins are good for frizzy hair because they have astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reduce oiliness, and lock in moisture, thereby smoothing and taming frizzy locks.

remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal teas for frizzy hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea that helps with frizzy hair, start by gathering dried Thymus serpyllum leaves, also known as wild thyme. You will need about a teaspoon of dried leaves for every cup of boiling water. First, heat water in a pot until it starts boiling, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Next, add the dried Thymus serpyllum leaves to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid into a cup to remove the leaves, then let it cool down. Once cooled, use the Thymus serpyllum tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. After shampooing, pour the cooled tea over your hair, focusing on the ends, and let it sit for 2-3 minutes before rinsing it out with cold water. This tea is said to help balance the pH of your scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea is good for frizzy hair because of its soothing and nourishing properties. The tea is made from the flowers of the lavender plant, which are rich in antioxidants and essential oils. These compounds help to calm the scalp and reduce inflammation, leading to healthier hair growth. The tea also contains compounds that help to balance the pH of the scalp, which can help to reduce frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the antioxidants in the tea help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness and frizz. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea can also help to improve circulation to the scalp, which can help to promote healthy hair growth and reduce frizz.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for frizzy hair because it helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticles, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Linalyl acetate helps to reduce frizzy hair by smoothing the cuticle layer, allowing for a more even and flat hair surface that is less prone to tangling and frizz.

Rosmarinic acid is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, reduce inflammation, and lock in moisture, leading to smoother and more manageable hair.
Caffeic acid helps to smooth and tame frizzy hair by protecting it from damage and breaking caused by free radicals, while also locking in moisture to keep hair looking healthy and shiny.
Apigenin is good for frizzy hair because it can help to calm down the hair's cuticle layer, making it smoother and less prone to frizz, and also helps in locking in moisture to hydrate the hair.

remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for frizzy hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea for frizzy hair, start by gathering fresh or dried thyme leaves, also known as Thymus serpyllum. If using fresh thyme, rinse the leaves with clean water and pat them dry with a paper towel to remove excess moisture. Next, combine 1 tablespoon of thyme leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids. Once cooled, you can use the thyme tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. This helps to balance your scalp's pH and reduce frizz, leaving your hair smoother and more manageable. For an even more effective treatment, you can store the cooled thyme tea in the fridge and use it as a hair mask by applying it to your hair and leaving it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a natural remedy that can help to tame frizzy hair. The tea is made from the leaves of the aloe plant, which contains vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that nourish the scalp and hair. The vitamins in aloe tea, such as vitamin E and vitamin B, help to lock in moisture and protect the hair from damage. This can help to reduce frizz and flyaways, leaving hair looking smoother and more manageable. Aloe tea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe an itchy scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair. When used as a pre-shampoo treatment or a hair mask, aloe tea can help to hydrate and calm the hair, making it easier to style and less prone to frizz. Regular use of aloe tea can also help to repair damaged hair and promote healthy hair growth.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Aloe-emodin has antioxidant properties that help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz by protecting the hair from damage caused by free radicals.
Anthraquinones are good for frizzy hair because they have antioxidant properties that help to lock in moisture and reduce damage to the hair cuticles, resulting in smoother and less frizzy hair.
Saponins are good for frizzy hair because they help to lock in moisture and reduce the cuticle's roughness, allowing hair to lie flat and appear smoother.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for frizzy hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea that helps with frizzy hair, start by gathering a handful of dried Thymus serpyllum leaves, also known as wild thyme. You can purchase it from a health food store or grow your own in a sunny spot. Combine 1 tablespoon of dried leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their fragrance and properties into the water. Strain the tea into a separate container, discarding the leaves. Once the tea has cooled, you can use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Pour the tea over your hair, working it from the roots to the ends, and let it sit for 5-10 minutes before rinsing it out with cold water. Repeat this process once or twice a week to help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm tea, is a great option for people with frizzy hair. The main reason is that it helps to balance the scalp's pH level, which is often the cause of frizz. When the scalp is out of balance, it leads to an overproduction of sebum, causing the hair to become greasy and frizzy. The antibacterial and antifungal properties in Melissa officinalis tea help to calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and preventing the buildup of dirt and oil. As a result, the hair becomes smoother and less prone to frizz. Additionally, the tea's antiseptic properties can help to soothe an itchy scalp, which can also contribute to frizzy hair. By using Melissa officinalis tea as a hair treatment, you can help to calm your scalp and reduce frizz, leaving your hair looking healthier and more manageable.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid, also known as Thyme, is good for frizzy hair because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair.

Apigenin is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth, resulting in smoother and more manageable locks.
Quercetin is good for frizzy hair because it helps to reduce inflammation in the scalp and protect hair cuticles from damage, resulting in smoother and more manageable locks.

Luteolin is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to breakage and frizz, and also helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture.

remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for frizzy hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea for frizzy hair, start by gathering dried Thymus serpyllum leaves, which you can find at health food stores or online. Begin by boiling 1 liter of water in a large pot. Then, add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Thymus serpyllum leaves to the boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. After the steeping time, strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container. Discard the Thymus serpyllum leaves and let the tea cool to room temperature. Once cooled, you can use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Pour the tea over your hair, from roots to ends, and let it sit for 5-10 minutes before rinsing with cold water. This process can help to smooth and tame frizzy hair, leaving it looking healthier and more manageable.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea is good for frizzy hair because it has ingredients that calm and smooth the hair cuticles. The tea contains thymol, a natural antifungal agent that reduces breakage and damage to the hair. Thymol also helps to lock in moisture, which is important for frizzy hair. When the hair is well-moisturized, it is less likely to frizz and become unmanageable. Thymus vulgaris tea also contains antioxidants that protect the hair from environmental damage and promote healthy hair growth. Drinking the tea can help to strengthen the hair from the inside out, which can lead to fewer frizz-causing split ends. Additionally, the tea's antifungal properties can help to control scalp irritation, which can contribute to frizzy hair.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for frizzy hair because it helps to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and protect the hair cuticle from damage, resulting in smoother, shinier, and more manageable hair.
Thymol is good for frizzy hair because it contains antifungal and moisturizing properties that help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz, leaving hair smooth and manageable.
Tannins are good for frizzy hair because they help to reduce frizz by forming a protective barrier on the hair's surface, keeping it smooth and shiny.

Polyphenols (also known as Thyme) help to reduce frizzy hair by providing antioxidant properties that protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, leading to smoother, shinier, and more manageable hair.

Carvacrol, a natural compound found in Thyme, helps to reduce frizzy hair by smoothing the cuticle and locking in moisture, making it a useful ingredient in hair care products to achieve a sleek and shiny appearance.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea that helps with frizzy hair, you will need to gather fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves, also known as wild thyme. First, identify a safe area where the wild thyme grows, and carefully pick a handful of leaves, making sure not to harm the plant. Next, rinse the leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, combine the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help to release the herbal properties of the wild thyme into the water. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature. Once the tea is ready, you can use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Pour the tea over your hair, focusing on the ends, and gently massage it in to help lock in moisture and reduce frizz.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea is made from the leaves of the plant nettle. It's rich in vitamins and minerals, especially vitamin C and iron. These nutrients help to strengthen hair follicles and promote healthy hair growth. Urtica dioica tea also contains antioxidants that protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals. When used as a hair rinse, it can help to reduce frizz by closing the cuticles of the hair and giving it a smooth, shiny appearance. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also soothe an itchy scalp and reduce dandruff. By using Urtica dioica tea as a hair treatment, you can help to tame frizzy hair and leave it looking healthier and more manageable.

Tannins are good for frizzy hair because they help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz by forming a protective barrier on the hair's surface, keeping it smooth and manageable.
Polyphenols are good for frizzy hair because they help protect the hair from damage, repair the cuticle, and strengthen the hair structure, leading to smoother and more manageable hair.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea for frizzy hair, start by gathering dried Thymus serpyllum leaves, also known as wild thyme. You will need one teaspoon of the dried leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water. Place the dried leaves in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup and pour boiling water over them. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal goodness to release into the water. Strain the tea and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature for your scalp. Once cooled, use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Pour the Thymus serpyllum tea over your hair, working from the roots to the ends, and let it sit for 2-3 minutes before rinsing thoroughly with warm water. By incorporating this herbal tea into your hair care routine, you may be able to help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata tea is good for frizzy hair because it helps to reduce frizz and add shine. The tea contains saponins, which are natural cleansers that gently remove dirt and product buildup from the hair. This helps to close the cuticle layer of the hair, making it smoother and less prone to frizz. Cassia auriculata tea also helps to balance the pH of the scalp, which can cause frizz when it becomes imbalanced. By using the tea as a final rinse after shampooing, you can help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz. Additionally, the tea's antifungal properties can help to prevent scalp irritations and infections that can cause frizz and other hair problems. Regular use of Cassia auriculata tea may also help to strengthen hair and improve its elasticity, making it less prone to breakage and frizz.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the hair from damage, reduce frizz, and add shine.
Kaempferol is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting smooth, healthy-looking locks.
Ellagic acid is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help to protect the hair from damage, smooth the cuticle, and reduce frizz by locking in moisture and reducing dryness.

Gallic acid is good for frizzy hair because it has antioxidant properties that help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing frizz and leaving hair smooth and manageable.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Thymus serpyllum herbal tea that can help with frizzy hair, start by gathering fresh or dried Thymus serpyllum leaves. If using fresh leaves, simply chop them finely and set aside. If using dried leaves, skip this step. Next, boil one cup of water in a pot and remove from heat. Add one teaspoon of chopped fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves or one-half teaspoon of dried leaves to the hot water. Let the leaves steep for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal goodness to infuse into the water. After the steeping time is up, strain the liquid using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth into a cup. Discard the leaves and let the tea cool down. Once cooled, you can use the Thymus serpyllum tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. This can help to balance your hair's pH levels and reduce frizz.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tea is a natural remedy that can help with frizzy hair. This tea is made from the leaves of the melaleuca plant, which is rich in antioxidants and essential oils. The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm irritated scalps and reduce dandruff. When applied to the hair, it can help lock in moisture and reduce frizz. The antioxidants in the tea can also help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness and brittleness. Additionally, the tea's antifungal properties can help control yeast growth on the scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for frizzy hair because they have antioxidant properties that help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, leading to smoother and healthier-looking hair.
Quercetin helps to reduce frizzy hair by protecting the hair cuticle from damage, smoothing the hair surface, and locking in moisture, resulting in softer, shinier, and more manageable hair.
Tannins, also known as wild thyme, are good for frizzy hair because they contain antioxidant and astringent properties that help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and add shine to the hair.

Polyphenols are good for frizzy hair because they help to strengthen the hair cuticle, lock in moisture, and protect it from damage caused by free radicals, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea that helps with frizzy hair, start by gathering dried Thymus serpyllum flowers or leaves, which are also known as wild thyme. You will need about one teaspoon of the dried herb for every cup of boiling water. Next, boil fresh water in a pot and then add the dried Thymus serpyllum. Let it steep for five to seven minutes to allow the herb's properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herb. Allow the tea to cool down before using it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. This will help to balance the pH level of your hair and scalp, reducing frizz and making your hair look smoother and shinier.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum tea, also known as wild thyme, is a natural remedy that can help to improve frizzy hair. The tea contains antioxidants and essential oils that help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz. Wild thyme is rich in vitamin E, which is known to nourish and protect the hair, making it stronger and more resilient. When consumed as a tea, the antioxidants and oils in wild thyme can help to balance the pH of the scalp, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause frizz. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to calm the hair follicles, reducing heat damage and preventing frizz from forming. Drinking wild thyme tea may also help to improve hair's elasticity, allowing it to bounce back into shape and reducing frizz.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for frizzy hair because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce frizz by protecting the hair cuticle from damage and locking in moisture.

Quercetin is good for frizzy hair because it helps to protect the hair from damage and inflammation, and also helps to strengthen the hair follicles and improve the overall health and shine of the hair.

Thymol is good for frizzy hair because it has antifungal and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy hair growth, while also acting as a natural hair refresher and smoothing agent.
Tannins help to reduce frizzy hair by creating a protective barrier around the hair shaft, keeping it smooth and manageable by reducing the absorption of moisture from the air.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus serpyllum herbal teas for frizzy hair
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Thymus serpyllum herbal tea that can help with frizzy hair, start by gathering a handful of fresh thyme leaves, also known as Thymus serpyllum, which is a common herb used in cooking. You can find it in most grocery stores or grow it in your garden. Next, dry the thyme leaves completely either by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down or by spreading them out on a paper towel and letting them air dry. Once the thyme is completely dry, combine 1 tablespoon of the dried thyme with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup, discarding the thyme leaves. After cooling the tea to a comfortable temperature, apply it to your hair as a final rinse after shampooing and conditioning. Leave the tea on your hair for 5-10 minutes before rinsing it out with cold water. This herbal tea is believed to help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

supplementary_best: To remedy frizzy hair, a combination of herbal teas from 'Rosmarinus officinalis', 'Lavandula angustifolia', 'Melaleuca alternifolia', and 'Cymbopogon citratus' can be effective. 'Rosmarinus officinalis' tea contains antioxidants that help repair damaged hair cuticles, reducing frizz and adding shine. 'Lavandula angustifolia' tea has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. 'Melaleuca alternifolia', also known as tea tree oil, is known for its antifungal properties, which can help control dandruff and other scalp issues that can cause frizz. 'Cymbopogon citratus', or lemongrass tea, has a natural astringent effect that helps to balance the scalp's pH, reducing frizz and adding smoothness to hair. When combined, these herbal teas provide a comprehensive solution to frizzy hair by repairing, soothing, controlling, and balancing the hair and scalp.
supplementary_avoid: If you have frizzy hair, some herbal teas you should avoid are those containing Sage (Salvia officinalis) and Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla). Sage tea can be too drying for your hair, as it contains compounds that strip your locks of their natural moisture. This can make your hair even frizzier and more prone to breakage. Chamomile tea, on the other hand, contains apigenin, a compound that can cause your hair to become brittle and more prone to frizz. Both teas can also cause irritation to the scalp, leading to dryness and itchiness, which can further contribute to frizzy hair.
supplementary_other_remedies: Coconut oil is a popular natural remedy for frizzy hair. It works by locking in moisture and creating a barrier on the hair's surface to prevent water from entering and causing frizz. Coconut oil's fatty acids also help to nourish and repair damaged hair, reducing the likelihood of frizz. To use coconut oil for frizzy hair, apply a small amount to the ends of your hair and work your way up to the roots. Leave it on for at least 30 minutes before washing it out with a gentle shampoo.  Apple cider vinegar is another natural remedy for frizzy hair. Its acidity helps to balance the pH of the hair, closing the cuticle and reducing frizz. It also adds shine and smoothness to the hair, making it look healthier and more manageable. To use apple cider vinegar for frizzy hair, mix it with water and apply it to the hair after shampooing. Leave it on for a few minutes before rinsing it out with cold water.  Egg whites can also help to tame frizzy hair. The protein in egg whites helps to repair damaged hair, while its acidity helps to close the cuticle and reduce frizz. To use egg whites for frizzy hair, beat an egg white and apply it to the hair after shampooing. Leave it on for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out with cold water.  Olive oil is another natural remedy for frizzy hair. Like coconut oil, it helps to lock in moisture and create a barrier on the hair's surface to prevent water from entering and causing frizz. It also helps to nourish and repair damaged hair, reducing the likelihood of frizz. To use olive oil for frizzy hair, apply a small amount to the ends of your hair and work your way up to the roots. Leave it on for at least 30 minutes before washing it out with a gentle shampoo.  These natural remedies can help to reduce frizz and make your hair look healthier and more manageable.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with frizzy hair often have dry scalp conditions like dandruff or eczema. This is because frizzy hair usually lacks moisture, leading to inflammation and irritation. Medicinal herbs like tea tree oil and aloe vera can help soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that fight off infections that can cause dandruff, while aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that calm irritated skin.  Another issue people with frizzy hair might experience is itchy skin, especially on the scalp, face, and neck. This itchy skin is often caused by dryness and irritation. Herbs like burdock root and licorice root can help to calm itchy skin. Burdock root has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and itching, while licorice root has anti-irritant properties that soothe dry, itchy skin.  Some people with frizzy hair also have problems with acne or rosacea, which can be triggered by dryness and irritation. Herbs like calendula and chamomile can help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with these conditions. Calendula has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that calm irritated skin, while chamomile has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that reduce redness and promote relaxation.  In addition, people with frizzy hair might experience hair loss or slow hair growth due to dryness and damage to the hair follicles. Herbs like saw palmetto and ginkgo biloba can help to promote hair growth and reduce hair loss. Saw palmetto has anti-androgen properties that block the production of hormones that can contribute to hair loss, while ginkgo biloba has antioxidant properties that promote blood flow to the scalp, helping to nourish and strengthen hair follicles.
